ops.title: Decision: B1 (route cookiesync bare reads through authkit) dropped — keychain-ACL wrinkle
ops.body:
**Decided 2026-07-17 (user, on a codex second opinion): DROP B1.** The post-ship plan's Part B1 — route cookiesync's two bare Safe-Storage reads (`readSafeStorage`, `internal/cookie/consent.go`) through a new authkit `vault-read` subcommand for a single named keychain surface — is **not achievable as a clean swap**, so it was dropped. No authkit v0.1.1, no cookiesync B1 release.

**Why (the ground truth codex surfaced):** macOS keychain ACL trust is keyed to the *accessing binary's code identity*, not "some CLI tool." Chromium "… Safe Storage" login-keychain items carry a per-item ACL trusting the browser; `/usr/bin/security find-generic-password -w` works non-interactively today only because a past attended "Always Allow" seated a trusted-application entry for the fixed Apple system binary `/usr/bin/security`. `authkit` is a distinct signed identity and does NOT inherit that grant — it needs its own attended "Always Allow" per Safe-Storage item.

- `ObtainKeyUnprompted` (routed-release path): SAFE to move — only runs on biometry hosts that already ran `vault-enroll` (a plain, non-suppressed `SecItemCopyMatching` that can seat authkit's grant under attended conditions).
- `bareOutcomes` (no-biometry fallback): UNSAFE — authkit's identity is never granted on no-biometry hosts, and a prompt-free read (`interactionNotAllowed = true`) can never show the "Always Allow" dialog to fix it → `errSecInteractionNotAllowed` forever, a permanent regression of a path that works today.

Full single-surface would require an attended one-time authkit bootstrap per browser on no-biometry hosts (+ a real no-biometry test Mac). Benefit was "consistency, not crypto"; the plan pre-flagged B1 as the droppable part. Net: dropped. `readSafeStorage` stays on `/usr/bin/security`; the biometry vault path already routes through authkit. See [[cc-sudo-invoker-pin-boundary]].

If ever revived: split by call site, and verify on a no-biometry Mac that cold `authkit vault-read` observes errSecInteractionNotAllowed and cold `authkit vault-enroll` shows the OS sheet naming "authkit" before shipping.
